mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server designed to facilitate calls to over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate multiple LLM services with features like cost tracking and load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/v1.30.0/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

kubectl version --client

```

> Download the kubectl binary.
> Make the kubectl binary executable.
> Move the binary to your PATH.
> Verify the installation of kubectl.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node configuration.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> Deploy LiteLLM using the provided YAML.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Enable autoscaling for LiteLLM.
> Deploy monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in the application.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the LiteLLM deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up any necessary data before upgrading. This process involves draining the nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.1/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the LiteLLM deployment.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not be running or kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the LiteLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the nodes in the GKE cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the LiteLLM configuration or dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or be accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the deployment YAML.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Running' state when everything is working.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
